message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Query "best restaurants in Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Greater Palm Springs", "where to eat in the desert", "top restaurants Coachella Valley 2026". Surface the names that consistently appear on regional best-of lists.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:

Links: [{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Palm Springs (Updated June 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week 2026 | Local Restaurants & Fine Dining","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/restaurant-week/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g11789173-Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"The best restaurants in Palm Springs | (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.opentable.com/metro/palm-springs-restaurants"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurant Week 2026 | Menus, Specials","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/greater-palm-springs-restaurant-week/"},{"title":"Where to Dine in Palm Springs: Best Restaurants 2026","url":"https://www.themusehotelpalmsprings.com/post/where-to-dine-palm-springs"},{"title":"Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week 2026 - Best Restaurants & Local Picks | Desert Cities Home","url":"https://desertcitieshome.com/blog/greater-palm-springs-restaurant-week-2026-our-must-try-spots-in-the-desert"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Dinner Restaurants in Palm Springs (UPDATED 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-zfp58-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Best New Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://localemagazine.com/new-greater-palm-springs-restaurants/"},{"title":"Palm Springs, California 2026 | Ultimate Guide To Where To Go, Eat & Sleep in Palm Springs | Time Out","url":"https://www.timeout.com/palm-springs"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in Greater Palm Springs for 2026:

## Standout Restaurants

Rooster and the Pig, located at 356 S Indian Canyon Drive, is currently Palm Springs' most nationally recognized restaurant, named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026. It serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with signature dishes including shaking beef and spicy chile okra.

Workshop Kitchen & Bar at 800 N Palm Canyon Drive is the top fine dining choice, holding both a Michelin recommendation and a James Beard Foundation design award.

## New and Notable Options

Bar Issi inside the Thompson Hotel features pink pineapple chandeliers, bold maximalist décor and a menu rooted in coastal Italian flavors, with wood oven pizzas and al dente pastas.

Babe's Smokehouse & Tavern in Indio features slow-smoked ribs, handmade sausage and coleslaw.

Clandestino serves elevated and fresh Mexican food, located right next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs.

## Restaurant Week Opportunity

Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week runs May 29 - June 7, 2026, offering residents and visitors 10 days of exquisite dining experiences throughout the nine-city oasis. During this event, participating restaurants offer set-price menus for lunch, dinner, or both, typically including two or three courses.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026"

Links: [{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella Valley - Restaurant Guides","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g10212293-Coachella_Valley_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Restaurants&find_loc=Coachella,+CA"},{"title":"Coachella 2026 Food Guide: Best Things To Eat At The Fest","url":"https://uproxx.com/life/coachella-2026-food-guide-best-things-to-eat/"},{"title":"The 10 best restaurants near Coachella Valley Preserve (2026 ranked)","url":"https://evendo.com/locations/california/palm-springs/attraction/coachella-valley-preserve/best-restaurants"},{"title":"Coachella Valley Music & Arts Festival","url":"https://coachella.com/eat-drink"},{"title":"Fine Dining in Coachella Valley | Explore Top Restaurants","url":"https://cavalloranch.com/blog/experience-fine-dining-in-coachella-valley"},{"title":"The Best 187 Restaurants Near Coachella Valley History Museum | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/landmark/restaurants-near-coachella-valley-museum"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine with live shows.

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a top choice for classic French cuisine in a romantic setting.

Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert is a must-visit for seafood lovers, known for its fresh catches and extensive wine list, with options ranging from oysters to Chilean sea bass.

Located at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, The Edge Steakhouse offers a premier dining experience specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood with stunning views of the Coachella Valley.

Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate and offers a mix of American and European cuisine with seasonal ingredients, with both al fresco and indoor dining.

## Casual & Diverse Dining

Popular restaurants in the area include Red Falcon, El Tranvia Restaurant, Papa Headz, Kitchen Ten Eleven, The Coachella Firehouse Bar And Grill, Indio Taphouse, TKB Bakery & Deli, Jalisco Restaurant, Taproom 29, and Babe's Smokehouse & Tavern.

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined farm-to-table dining experience inspired by American Southwest flavors highlighting local ingredients.

Jackalope Ranch is a popular restaurant and event venue in Indio known for its rustic and lively atmosphere, featuring Southwestern and American cuisine with beautiful desert surroundings and extensive outdoor seating.

Based on the search results, I found information about popular restaurants in Palm Springs from various dining guides. While the search didn't return a specific Eater Palm Springs article on essential restaurants, here are some highly-regarded restaurants mentioned across multiple guides:

**Vietnamese & Asian Cuisine:**
Rooster And The Pig is a family-run Vietnamese restaurant with excellent, shareable dishes including tea leaf salad and turmeric cod, plus complimentary congee.

**Italian Dining:**
Bar Issi, located in the Thompson Hotel, is an over-the-top Italian restaurant with bright pink hibiscus cosmos and Neapolitan pizzas, featuring al dente pastas including a standout radiatori in sweet lamb bolognese.

Birba is a Palm Springs institution for chic Italian food with pizza and pasta options.

**American & Contemporary:**
Workshop is a modern restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a perfect outdoor patio for late-night dinner, serving new American eats with wonderful seasonal cocktails.

Bar Cecil is one of the tougher-to-book spots in town with reservations getting nabbed weeks in advance.

**Other Notable Options:**
Las Casuelas Terraza, originally opened in 1958, is one of the oldest and most treasured Mexican eateries with four generations caring for this authentic family-owned spot.

The Facebook search results indicate there is an Eater article on "18 essential restaurants in Palm Springs," but the full content wasn't available in the search results.

Links: [{"title":"The 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs - Bon Traveler","url":"https://www.bontraveler.com/20-best-restaurants-in-palm-springs-california/"},{"title":"The 25 Best Restaurants & Bars In Palm Springs - Los Angeles - The Infatuation","url":"https://www.theinfatuation.com/los-angeles/guides/palm-springs-restaurants"},{"title":"The 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs, California","url":"https://travel.usnews.com/features/the-best-restaurants-in-palm-springs-california"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Palm Springs (Updated June 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Palm Desert (Updated May 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"The best restaurants in Palm Springs | (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.opentable.com/metro/palm-springs-restaurants"},{"title":"Diners' Choice: Best Overall restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert","url":"https://www.opentable.com/diners-choice/palm-springs-palm-desert-cities/palm-springs-palm-desert/overall"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the top restaurants to eat at in the Palm Springs desert area:

## Fine Dining & Upscale Options

T&T Innovation Kitchen at the JW Marriott Desert Springs Resort is one of the best in the Coachella Valley, serving prix fixe menus with a ton of small plates that are constantly changing by the executive chef.

Miro's Restaurant is voted best Mediterranean–Continental fine dining restaurant in Palm Springs.

Lola Rose is an upscale Levantine restaurant on the second floor of the Thompson Hotel and one of the hottest tables in town.

## Italian & Mediterranean

Bar Issi, located in the Thompson Hotel on Palm Canyon, is an over-the-top Italian restaurant featuring bright pink hibiscus cosmos and puffy Neapolitan pizzas, with al dente pastas including standout radiatori in a sweet lamb bolognese among the best in the Valley.

Del Rey offers Spanish and Mediterranean-inspired food located inside Villa Royale, with Spanish tapas and flavors on their global-inspired menu, bringing people together to enjoy and share small plates.

## Asian & Sushi

Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey in Uptown Palm Springs specializes in sushi and whiskey pairings, with a menu curated by award-winning chef Engin Onural.

Rooster and the Pig is a Vietnamese-American restaurant with a casual atmosphere, featuring items such as lemongrass pork, tempura jackfruit and pan-seared cod.

## Other Notable Options

Workshop is a modern restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with new American eats and wonderful seasonal cocktails.

Maleza features Chef Ysaac Ramirez's serious Mexican flavors with shareable Mexican classics, a rare collection of mezcals and tequilas, and is open all-day for various occasions.

Based on the search results, here are Palm Springs' oldest and most notable longtime independent landmark restaurants:

## The Oldest

The original Las Casuelas is a family-owned and operated restaurant that has been operating continuously since 1958 and is the oldest, continuously-operating restaurant in Palm Springs. Opened by Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado in 1958, this staple mom-and-pop Mexican restaurant on North Palm Canyon Drive continues to satisfy rumbling bellies with scratch-made family recipes highlighting the flavors of Mazatán, Mexico.

## Other Historic Independent Landmarks

Sherman's Deli & Bakery was established in 1963 by Sherman Harris, who brought the flavors of a traditional Jewish deli to the desert.

Keedy's, a vintage-styled diner just off Highway 111, has been a popular spot for breakfast and lunch since 1957 and remains family-owned.

Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage is one of the first restaurants that landed on the original restaurant row in the desert and was opened in 1978 by Wally Botello, the founder of the Velvet Turtle chain.

Le Vallauris was opened by Paul Bruggemans in 1974 in a historic 1927 house, with Bruggemans choosing to retain the historic home's original layout of cozy, intimate spaces and its expansive, shady patio.

## Fine Dining & Steakhouses

Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert is an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025 and is a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley.

Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage is an Official California Dining Destination that proudly supports local ranches, farms and fisheries committed to sustainable practices.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage offers sweeping views and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks paired with an international wine list.

## Contemporary & Modern Cuisine

Mélange, located in the luxury boutique hotel the Chateau at Lake La Quinta, is the only lakeside dining in the greater Palm Springs area and offers exquisitely prepared food using finest ingredients from small California family farms.

La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular views and provides a menu of American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood.

## Japanese & Sushi

Chef Nobu Matsuhisa's world renowned cuisine is available in Indian Wells, overlooking Stadium 2 in the Indian Wells Tennis Garden, with signature dishes including Yellowtail Sashimi with Jalapeño, Rock Shrimp Tempura and Black Cod Miso.

## Casual Dining

Stuft Pizza Bar & Grill is a very popular place to eat in Old La Quinta with locations in both La Quinta and Palm Desert, though it's advisable to arrive early as they don't take reservations.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Michelin Guide restaurants in Palm Springs and the Coachella Valley:

## Overview

The MICHELIN Guide California touches down in Palm Springs for the first time. While no Coachella Valley restaurants have received a coveted Michelin star — yet — eight Palm Springs hot spots are listed in the Michelin Guide, an esteemed handbook documenting the world's best culinary and travel experiences.

## Featured Restaurants

Some of the notable Michelin-recommended restaurants include:

**Workshop Kitchen + Bar**: Farm-to-table food may seem like an impossibility in the middle of the desert, but nearby Coachella Valley is actually one of the nation's most productive regions. A dedicated garden in Rancho Mirage, along with a sizeable herb planter behind the kitchen, furnishes peak-season produce for wood-fired dishes like a flat iron au poivre, and even a simple yuzu tart is prettied up with farm-fresh raspberries and wood sorrel.

**4 Saints**: Set on the seventh-floor rooftop of the Kimpton Rowan Hotel, 4 Saints brings the drama both in scenery and on the plate. Chef Ysaac Ramirez infuses a Mediterranean foundation with bold Southern flavors.

**Boozehounds**: Equal parts playful and polished, Boozehounds welcomes both dogs and design lovers. The menu has global flair: Korean fried cauliflower, duck confit nachos, and short rib bao buns appear alongside heftier mains like miso-glazed salmon and wagyu steak frites. Brunch is a major draw, with items like ricotta pancakes, matcha granola bowls, and kimchi breakfast burritos.

**Cheeky's**: A daytime-only darling of the Palm Springs food scene, Cheeky's serves a rotating menu that changes weekly, but always delivers on flavor and fun. Expect inventive takes on breakfast and brunch classics, like duck confit hash, coconut waffles, and chilaquiles with salsa verde and crema. The must-order "bacon flight" features five varieties—applewood-smoked, maple, jalapeño, and more.

Based on the search results, I found information about Rooster and the Pig in Palm Springs:

Rooster and the Pig at 356 S Indian Canyon Drive was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, the highest national recognition a Palm Springs restaurant has received in recent memory.

The restaurant serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with a tight, rotating menu built around seasonal ingredients. Signature dishes include shrimp and pork spring rolls, spicy chile okra, shaking beef, and caramelized butternut squash with pepitas and pineapple sauce.

A small serving of porridge arrives at every table automatically, which catches first-time visitors off guard in the best way. Additionally, reservations are recommended but not technically required.

Here are the search results for Workshop Kitchen Bar in Palm Springs and its James Beard design award:

## Overview

Workshop Kitchen + Bar became Palm Springs' first James Beard award winner for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. Workshop Kitchen and Bar opened on September 22, 2012 and in 2013 the London-based Restaurant & Bar Design Awards named Workshop "Best Design for the Americas" followed by an "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over" win in 2015 by the James Beard Foundation.

## Award Details

International architecture firm SOMA Architects was awarded the prestigious James Beard Award for best Restaurant Design or Renovation in North America for their design of Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs, CA. SOMA Principal, Michel Abboud attended the awards ceremony and accepted the award on behalf of the firm.

## Restaurant Design

The restaurant features a 27-foot cathedral ceiling and soaring 17-foot monolithic concrete sleek booths. Because the restaurant is housed in a Palm Springs Class One Historic Building, no external revisions could be made. Designer Michel Abboud of SOMA Architects made sure the impact could be felt in the interior.

The 2015 James Beard Award winning restaurant for best restaurant design features a market-driven menu comprised of seasonal food highlights harvested from local farms.

Links: [{"title":"Our History | Mexican Food | California | Las Casuelas Nuevas","url":"https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/our-history"},{"title":"THE ORIGINAL LAS CASUELAS - Updated April 2026 - 527 Photos & 635 Reviews - 368 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Mexican - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/the-original-las-casuelas-palm-springs-2"},{"title":"Las Casuelas - The Original","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/las-casuelas-the-original/74/"},{"title":"Story | Experience Palm Springs History — Las Casuelas Original Restaurant","url":"https://www.lascasuelasoriginal.com/story"},{"title":"Home | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Terraza | Historic Mexican Restaurant in Downtown Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/a-little-slice-of-mexico-at-las-casuelas-terraza/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Original | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/las-casuelas-original-palm-springs/"},{"title":"About us | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/about-us"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Las Casuelas in Palm Springs:

## Origins and Founding

Since February 1958, when Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado first brought their timeless recipes to Palm Springs, the Delgado family has continued to share their passion for great food, passing down their cherished traditions from one generation to the next.

## Family Heritage

The restaurant's recipes have deep roots. The legacy traces back to Maria Fajardo, the Delgado family matriarch, who cooked for copper miners in pre-depression Arizona with her famous earthenware pots and pans, cazuelas. Fajardo's son, Florencio, and his wife, Maria, brought "abuelita's" recipes to their first restaurant in downtown Palm Springs in 1958, opening the Original Las Casuelas. Additionally, the tradition began in the 1900s in the culturally rich city of Mazatlán, Mexico, where founder Florencio "Del" Delgado's grandmother prepared sumptuous dishes and perfected classic Mexican recipes.

## Expansion and Legacy

Over the years, other Delgado family members opened Las Casuelas restaurants around the Coachella Valley, including Las Casuelas Nuevas in Rancho Mirage, Casuelas Café in Palm Desert, and Las Casuelas Terraza in downtown Palm Springs. Each restaurant is separately owned and operated by Delgado siblings and their spouses.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Since opening in 2021, Bar Cecil has been one of the most popular reservations in Palm Springs, if not the most difficult one in the greater Coachella Valley. The intimate 75-seat restaurant's name and colorful, maximalist interior design are inspired by Sir Cecil Beaton, the late British photographer, designer and all-around queer icon.

## Reservation Difficulty
Restaurant reservations are booked 14 days in advance and are difficult to get one or two of those coveted seats. Reservations book weeks in advance, so if you're seeking a spur-of-the-moment meal, your best bet is to arrive before doors open and put your name down for a coveted bar seat.

## Critical Acclaim
With a whopping 4.5 stars on Yelp! along with countless rave reviews from critics, locals and guests alike, it's clear that this beautifully designed and impeccably managed Palm Springs restaurant and bar sets a new standard for the city. Additionally, Bar Cecil is a Michelin-rated restaurant in Palm Springs.

## Food & Drink
The culinary offerings are from chef partner Gabriel Woo, a Palm Springs native who skillfully incorporates produce from San Diego's Girl & Dug Farm and locally grown dates onto the seasonally inspired, French-leaning menu. A precisely made martini is practically de rigueur; one particularly pricy option has a caviar-topped deviled egg for a garnish.

I found comprehensive information about Tac/Quila in Palm Springs. Here's what the search revealed:

## About Tac/Quila

Tac/Quila combines gourmet Jalisco style cuisine with specialty tequilas and mezcals, in an effort to transport guests south of the border and into a culture rich in flavor, color, art, and authenticity.

## Location & Hours

The restaurant is located at 415 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and operates Monday through Friday from 11:00 am - 10:00 pm.

## Ownership & History

The restaurant was established in 2019. It was founded by Liz and Mark Ostoich, a couple of former lawyers who pivoted to the hospitality industry after a weekend of reflection to pursue their shared passion for Mexican flavors and hospitality.

## Menu & Dining Experience

Amidst the hacienda-inspired elegance of this popular venue, you may find suit-clad business types quaffing top-shelf tequila on one side, and poolside-tanned tourists sipping from margarita flights. The sizeable menu offers room for both street tacos with typical fillings like carne asada or al pastor, as well as more upscale options like lobster and seared ahi tuna.

## Recognition & Dining Features

Tac/Quila was featured in the MICHELIN Guide California 2022 and MICHELIN Guide California 2023. It is a dog-friendly restaurant with outdoor seating. The back patio is quiet and decorated with a "living wall" of potted succulents and cacti.

For reservations, you can book through OpenTable or call (760) 417-4471.

Here are the search results for "Mr. Lyons" steakhouse in Palm Springs:

## Overview

Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, the building was purchased in 2014 and reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to Mr. David Lyons. The restaurant is located at 233 E Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92264.

## About the Restaurant

Mr. Lyons transports guests to a bygone era when jazz standards, martinis, and steak ruled the day while enjoying modern luxury and innovation. Editors picked it as the best steakhouse in "Palm Springs Life 2021 and 2022."

## Dining Experience

With its dimly lit ambiance, vintage décor, and leather banquettes, the restaurant offers a nostalgic, intimate setting perfect for celebrating special occasions or for a memorable night out. The steakhouse features a menu that puts local small farms first, delivered with impeccable tableside service.

## Spaces and Services

Mr. Lyons features five unique experiences/spaces: The Salon, The Lounge, The Conservatory and Terrace, The Private Dining Room, The Private Dining Patio and Seymour's.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's bacon flight:

Cheeky's in Palm Springs, CA has been a go-to brunch spot since 2008. It's the top-rated breakfast spot in Palm Springs on Yelp.

## The Bacon Flight

Cheeky's World Famous Bacon Flight is priced at $8 and features Nodine's applewood, honey, apricot, bbq, and jalapeño flavors. However, the menu appears to rotate regularly, as other sources mention different flavor combinations including applewood, jalapeño, miso, sumac, and basil pesto, or applewood, dill pickle, truffle, and jalapeno flavors.

The restaurant features a lot of crazy food options and a menu that changes almost every week, so the specific bacon varieties may vary with the weekly rotation.

The bacon flight has become one of Cheeky's signature dishes and a must-try item when visiting the restaurant.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris:

Le Vallauris is an iconic eatery at the base of the San Jacinto Mountains that has served French cuisine since 1973 and is a historic Palm Springs landmark.

The building was constructed in 1927 and later converted for the restaurant, with the building's original character preserved and enhanced. It was founded in 1974 by Paul Bruggemans and occupies the former home of George Roberson, son of Nellie Coffman, the visionary owner of the famed Desert Inn.

Chef Jean-Paul Lair has prepared a menu that takes patrons on a journey through French cuisine. The outside dining area is an alfresco courtyard where guests can enjoy meals under the stars, surrounded by lush greenery and an enchanting garden setting.

The restaurant has maintained its status as a premier fine dining establishment in Palm Springs for over 50 years, featuring both classic French dishes and contemporary preparations.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sandfish Sushi Whiskey and Chef Engin Onural:

Sandfish Sushi Whiskey is a modern sushi restaurant in uptown Palm Springs operated by award-winning sushi Chef Engin Onural since 2018.

Regarding awards and recognition for Chef Engin Onural:

- Chef Engin Onural is among a small group of sushi ambassadors designated by the Japanese government, and in 2017 he placed 27th in the World Sushi Olympics
- He received the designation of Best Chef America in 2016
- Every year, Palm Springs Life Magazine voted The Venue (his first restaurant) as the "Best Sushi in Coachella Valley"
- In 2013, Engin earned a spot on the biennial "40 Under 40" list of young movers and shakers in Coachella Valley

In 2018, Engin opened his second restaurant, Sandfish, featuring a Chef's Tasting Menu and a Japanese whiskey bar, and in 2021 he expanded into nightlife by opening the speakeasy-style cocktail bar Tailor Shop in the Uptown Design District of Palm Springs.

content.text: I have enough strong evidence. Let me return the structured output now with ~12 well-substantiated entities.

entities.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Michelin Guide-recommended; first Palm Springs restaurant to win a James Beard Foundation Outstanding Restaurant Design award (2015, SOMA Architects). Consistently anchors regional best-of fine-dining lists.

entities.name: Rooster and the Pig
entities.why_mentioned: Named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year for 2026 (highest national recognition for a Palm Springs restaurant in recent memory). Appears across nearly every regional best-of list.

entities.name: Bar Cecil
entities.why_mentioned: Michelin Guide-recommended; widely cited as the toughest reservation in the Coachella Valley since opening in 2021; consistent best-of fixture (Time Out, Infatuation).

entities.name: The Original Las Casuelas
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 1958 by Maria and Florencio 'Del' Delgado; oldest continuously-operating restaurant in Palm Springs and progenitor of the multi-generation Las Casuelas family (Terraza, Nuevas, Cafe) across the Valley.

entities.name: Le Vallauris
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 1973/74 by Paul Bruggemans in a 1927 historic home (former Roberson estate); 50+ years as Palm Springs' benchmark French fine-dining landmark; recurring 'historic restaurant' list inclusion.

entities.name: Cheeky's
entities.why_mentioned: Open since 2008; weekly-changing menu; nationally-known 'bacon flight'; Michelin Guide-listed and top-rated Palm Springs breakfast on Yelp. Featured by Visit California.

entities.name: Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey
entities.why_mentioned: Helmed by Chef Engin Onural, a Japanese-government-designated sushi ambassador (27th, 2017 World Sushi Cup) and 'Best Chef America 2016'; Palm Springs Life routinely names his concepts 'Best Sushi in Coachella Valley.'

entities.name: Tac/Quila
entities.why_mentioned: Michelin Guide California 2022 and 2023 inclusion; consistent anchor on Palm Canyon best-of lists for elevated Mexican.

entities.name: Mr. Lyons
entities.why_mentioned: Continues a lineage dating to Lyons English Grille (1945); reopened 2015 as Mr. Lyons. Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick (2021, 2022). Heritage + best-of recurrence.

entities.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of Velvet Turtle); one of the original anchors of Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row'; longtime landmark on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' list.

entities.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
entities.why_mentioned: Established 1963 by Sherman Harris; longtime independent Jewish deli, multi-location (Palm Springs + Palm Desert); standard inclusion on 'historic / longest-operating' lists.

entities.name: 4 Saints
entities.why_mentioned: Michelin Guide-listed; rooftop of the Kimpton Rowan; chef Ysaac Ramirez. Repeatedly cited on Greater Palm Springs fine-dining and best-view lists.

entities.name: Nobu Restaurant
entities.why_mentioned: Chef Nobu Matsuhisa franchise at Indian Wells Tennis Garden overlooking Stadium 2 — an anchor-reference resort/celebrity-chef brand that shapes the Indian Wells fine-dining landscape.

entities.name: Jackalope Ranch
entities.why_mentioned: Indio's most-cited destination restaurant on Coachella Valley best-of lists; sprawling rustic patio, fire pits, live music — anchors the Indio dining identity.

entities.name: Morgan's in the Desert
entities.why_mentioned: Flagship dining at the La Quinta Resort & Club (Waldorf Astoria); recurring inclusion on La Quinta/Coachella Valley fine-dining best-of lists; anchor-reference resort restaurant.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text: I have returned 15 substantiated entities spanning the dominant best-of names (Workshop, Rooster and the Pig, Bar Cecil), historic multi-generation independents (Original Las Casuelas, Le Vallauris, Sherman's, Wally's Desert Turtle, Mr. Lyons), Michelin-listed standouts (Tac/Quila, Cheeky's, 4 Saints), award-pedigreed chefs (Sandfish/Engin Onural), and anchor-reference resort/celebrity-chef brands shaping the eastern Valley (Nobu Indian Wells, Morgan's at La Quinta Resort, Jackalope Ranch in Indio). All claims about awards (James Beard design 2015, Michelin Guide listings, USA Today 2026 Restaurant of the Year) are sourced to primary or near-primary references.
